   103→export class SessionStore {
   104→  private configPath: string
   105→  private backupPath: string
   106→  private data: StoredData
   107→
   108→  constructor() {
   109→    const configDir = join(app.getPath('userData'), 'config')
   110→    if (!existsSync(configDir)) {
   111→      mkdirSync(configDir, { recursive: true })
   112→    }
   113→    this.configPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json')
   114→    this.backupPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json.backup')
   115→    this.data = this.load()
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  private loadFile(path: string): StoredData | null {
   119→    try {
   120→      if (!existsSync(path)) return null
   121→      const stat = statSync(path)
   122→      if (stat.size === 0) return null
   123→      const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
   124→      const data = JSON.parse(content)
   125→      if (!data?.workspace) return null
   126→      return data
   127→    } catch (e) {
   128→      console.error(`[SessionStore] Failed to load ${path}:`, e)
   129→      return null
   130→    }
   131→  }
   132→
   133→  private load(): StoredData {
   134→    // Try main file first
   135→    const main = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
   136→    if (main && (main.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   137→      return main
   138→    }
   139→
   140→    // Main file is empty/corrupt/missing — try backup
   141→    const backup = this.loadFile(this.backupPath)
   142→    if (backup && (backup.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   143→      console.log('[SessionStore] Main config empty/corrupt, restored from backup (' +
   144→        backup.workspace.projects.length + ' projects)')
   145→      // Restore backup as main file immediately
   146→      try {
   147→        writeFileSync(this.configPath, JSON.stringify(backup, null, 2))
   148→      } catch (e) {
   149→        console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to restore backup to main:', e)
   150→      }
   151→      return backup
   152→    }
   153→
   154→    // If main loaded but had 0 projects (valid empty state), use it
   155→    if (main) return main
   156→
   157→    return {
   158→      workspace: {
   159→        projects: [],
   160→        openTabs: [],
   161→        activeTabId: null
   162→      }
   163→    }
   164→  }
   165→
   166→  private save(): void {
   167→    try {
   168→      const json = JSON.stringify(this.data, null, 2)
   169→
   170→      // Validate we can parse what we're about to write
   171→      JSON.parse(json)
   172→
   173→      // Back up existing file before overwriting (if it has content)
   174→      if (existsSync(this.configPath)) {
   175→        try {
   176→          const stat = statSync(this.configPath)
   177→          if (stat.size > 0) {
   178→            // Only update backup if existing file is valid and has projects
   179→            const existing = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
   180→            if (existing && (existing.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   181→              writeFileSync(this.backupPath, readFileSync(this.configPath))
   182→            }
   183→          }
   184→        } catch (e) {
   185→          console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to update backup:', e)
   186→        }
   187→      }
   188→
   189→      // Atomic write: write to temp file, then rename
   190→      const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
   191→      writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
   192→
   193→      // Verify the temp file was written completely
   194→      const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
   195→      JSON.parse(written) // throws if truncated/corrupt
   196→
   197→      renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)
   198→    } catch (e) {
   199→      console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to save workspace:', e)
   200→    }
   201→  }
   202→
   203→  getWorkspace(): Workspace {
   204→    return this.data.workspace
   205→  }
   206→
   207→  // Reload workspace from disk (useful when file was modified externally)
   208→  reloadFromDisk(): void {
   209→    console.log('[SessionStore] Reloading workspace from disk')
   210→    this.data = this.load()
   211→    console.log('[SessionStore] Reloaded, projects:', this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0)
   212→  }
   213→
   214→  saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): void {
   215→    // Protect against overwriting populated workspace with empty one
   216→    const incomingProjects = workspace?.projects?.length || 0
   217→    const currentProjects = this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
   218→    if (incomingProjects === 0 && currentProjects > 0) {
   219→      console.log('[SessionStore] Rejected empty workspace save - current has', currentProjects, 'projects')
   220→      return
   221→    }
   222→    this.data.workspace = workspace
   223→    this.save()
   224→    syncMetaProjects(workspace)
   225→  }
   226→
   227→  getWindowBounds(): WindowBounds | undefined {
   228→    return this.data.windowBounds
   229→  }
   230→
   231→  saveWindowBounds(bounds: WindowBounds): void {
   232→    this.data.windowBounds = bounds
   233→    this.save()
   234→  }
   235→
   236→  getSettings(): Settings {
   237→    return this.data.settings ?? { defaultProjectDir: '', theme: 'default', backend: 'default' }
   238→  }
   239→

     4→
     5→import { WebSocket, WebSocketServer } from 'ws'
     6→import { Server } from 'http'
     7→import { log } from './utils'
     8→import { PendingFile } from './types'
     9→
    10→export interface WebSocketManagerDeps {
    11→  getToken: () => string
    12→  getPtyManager: () => any
    13→  getPort: () => number
    14→  getTerminalSubscriptions: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    15→  getPtyStreams: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    16→  getPtyDataBuffer: () => Map<string, string[]>
    17→  getConnectedClients: () => Set<WebSocket>

    19→}

    21→export function setupWebSocket(server: Server, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): WebSocketServer {
    22→  const wss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
    23→
    24→  // Handle upgrade requests manually to support multiple paths
    25→  server.on('upgrade', (req, socket, head) => {
    26→    const url = new URL(req.url || '', `http://localhost:${deps.getPort()}`)
    27→    const pathname = url.pathname
    28→
    29→    // Get token from Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header (preferred) or query string (fallback)
    30→    const protocolHeader = req.headers['sec-websocket-protocol'] as string | undefined
    31→    const protocols = protocolHeader?.split(',').map(p => p.trim()) || []
    32→    const tokenFromProtocol = protocols.find(p => p.startsWith('token-'))?.slice(6)
    33→    const tokenFromQuery = url.searchParams.get('token')
    34→    const token = tokenFromProtocol || tokenFromQuery
    35→
    36→    log('WebSocket upgrade request', {
    37→      url: req.url,
    38→      pathname,
    39→      hasProtocolToken: !!tokenFromProtocol,
    40→      hasQueryToken: !!tokenFromQuery,
    41→      headers: {
    42→        host: req.headers.host,
    43→        origin: req.headers.origin,
    44→        upgrade: req.headers.upgrade
    45→      }
    46→    })
    47→
    48→    // Validate token for all WebSocket connections
    49→    if (token !== deps.getToken()) {
    50→      log('WebSocket auth failed', { providedToken: token?.slice(0, 8) })
    51→      socket.write('HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized\r\n\r\n')
    52→      socket.destroy()
    53→      return
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    // Handle PTY stream WebSocket: /api/pty/:id/stream
    57→    const ptyStreamMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/pty\/([^/]+)\/stream$/)
    58→    if (ptyStreamMatch) {
    59→      const ptyId = ptyStreamMatch[1]
    60→      handlePtyStreamUpgrade(req, socket, head, ptyId, deps)
    61→      return
    62→    }
    63→
    64→    // Handle main WebSocket path: /ws
    65→    if (pathname === '/ws') {
    66→      wss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
    67→        wss.emit('connection', ws, req)
    68→      })
    69→      return
    70→    }
    71→
    72→    // Unknown WebSocket path
    73→    log('Unknown WebSocket path', { pathname })
    74→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
    75→    socket.destroy()
    76→  })
    77→
    78→  wss.on('connection', (ws: WebSocket, _req) => {
    79→    log('WebSocket client connected (main)')
    80→
    81→    deps.getConnectedClients().add(ws)
    82→
    83→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
    84→      try {
    85→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
    86→        handleWebSocketMessage(ws, msg, deps)
    87→      } catch (e) {
    88→        log('Invalid WebSocket message', { error: String(e) })
    89→      }
    90→    })
    91→
    92→    ws.on('close', () => {
    93→      deps.getConnectedClients().delete(ws)
    94→
    95→      deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().forEach((subscribers, ptyId) => {
    96→        subscribers.delete(ws)
    97→        if (subscribers.size === 0) {
    98→          deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().delete(ptyId)
    99→        }
   100→      })
   101→      log('WebSocket client disconnected (main)')
   102→    })
   103→
   104→    // Send welcome message
   105→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   106→
   107→    // Send any pending files to new client
   108→    sendPendingFilesToClient(ws, deps.getPendingFiles())
   109→  })
   110→
   111→  return wss
   112→}
   113→
   114→function handlePtyStreamUpgrade(
   115→  req: any,
   116→  socket: any,
   117→  head: any,
   118→  ptyId: string,
   119→  deps: WebSocketManagerDeps
   120→): void {
   121→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   122→  if (!ptyManager || !ptyManager.getProcess(ptyId)) {
   123→    log('PTY stream upgrade failed - PTY not found', { ptyId })
   124→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
   125→    socket.destroy()
   126→    return
   127→  }
   128→
   129→  const streamWss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
   130→
   131→  streamWss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
   132→    log('PTY stream connected', { ptyId })
   133→
   134→    const ptyStreams = deps.getPtyStreams()
   135→    if (!ptyStreams.has(ptyId)) {
   136→      ptyStreams.set(ptyId, new Set())
   137→    }
   138→    ptyStreams.get(ptyId)!.add(ws)
   139→
   140→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', ptyId }))
   141→
   142→    // Flush any buffered data
   143→    flushPtyBuffer(ptyId, ws, deps.getPtyDataBuffer())
   144→
   145→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
   146→      try {
   147→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
   148→
   149→        switch (msg.type) {
   150→          case 'input':
   151→            if (msg.data && ptyManager) {
   152→              ptyManager.write(ptyId, msg.data)
   153→            }
   154→            break
   155→
   156→          case 'resize':
   157→            if (msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   158→              ptyManager.resize(ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   159→            }
   160→            break
   161→
   162→          case 'ping':
   163→            ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   164→            break
   165→
   166→          default:
   167→            log('Unknown PTY stream message type', { type: msg.type, ptyId })
   168→        }
   169→      } catch (e) {
   170→        log('Invalid PTY stream message', { error: String(e), ptyId })
   171→      }
   172→    })
   173→
   174→    ws.on('close', () => {
   175→      log('PTY stream disconnected', { ptyId })
   176→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   177→      if (ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.size === 0) {
   178→        ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   179→      }
   180→    })
   181→
   182→    ws.on('error', (err) => {
   183→      log('PTY stream error', { error: String(err), ptyId })
   184→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   185→    })
   186→  })
   187→}
   188→
   189→function handleWebSocketMessage(ws: WebSocket, msg: any, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): void {
   190→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   191→  const terminalSubscriptions = deps.getTerminalSubscriptions()
   192→
   193→  switch (msg.type) {
   194→    case 'subscribe':
   195→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   196→        if (!terminalSubscriptions.has(msg.ptyId)) {
   197→          terminalSubscriptions.set(msg.ptyId, new Set())
   198→        }
   199→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)!.add(ws)
   200→        ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'subscribed', ptyId: msg.ptyId }))
   201→      }
   202→      break
   203→
   204→    case 'unsubscribe':
   205→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   206→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   207→      }
   208→      break
   209→
   210→    case 'write':
   211→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.data && ptyManager) {
   212→        ptyManager.write(msg.ptyId, msg.data)
   213→      }
   214→      break
   215→
   216→    case 'resize':
   217→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   218→        ptyManager.resize(msg.ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   219→      }
   220→      break
   221→
   222→    case 'ping':
   223→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   224→      break
   225→  }
   226→}
   227→
   228→function flushPtyBuffer(ptyId: string, ws: WebSocket, ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>): void {
   229→  const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)
   230→  if (!buffer || buffer.length === 0) return
   231→
   232→  buffer.forEach(data => {
   233→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   234→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'data', data }))
   235→    }
   236→  })
   237→
   238→  ptyDataBuffer.delete(ptyId)
   239→}
   240→
   241→function sendPendingFilesToClient(ws: WebSocket, pendingFiles: Map<string, PendingFile>): void {
   242→  const now = Date.now()
   243→  // Clean up expired files first
   244→  for (const [fileId, file] of pendingFiles) {
   245→    if (file.expiresAt < now) {
   246→      pendingFiles.delete(fileId)
   247→    }
   248→  }
   249→
   250→  const files = Array.from(pendingFiles.values()).map(f => ({
   251→    id: f.id,
   252→    name: f.name,
   253→    size: f.size,
   254→    mimeType: f.mimeType,
   255→    message: f.message
   256→  }))
   257→
   258→  if (files.length > 0) {
   259→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'files:pending', files }))
   260→  }
   261→}
   262→
   263→export function broadcastTerminalData(
   264→  ptyId: string,
   265→  data: string,
   266→  terminalSubscriptions: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
   267→): void {
   268→  const subscribers = terminalSubscriptions.get(ptyId)
   269→  if (!subscribers) return
   270→
   271→  const message = JSON.stringify({
   272→    type: 'terminal-data',
   273→    ptyId,
   274→    data
   275→  })
   276→
   277→  subscribers.forEach(ws => {
   278→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   279→      ws.send(message)
   280→    }
   281→  })
   282→}
   283→
   284→export function broadcastPtyData(
   285→  ptyId: string,
   286→  data: string,
   287→  ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
   288→  ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>
   289→): void {
   290→  const streams = ptyStreams.get(ptyId)
   291→
   292→  // If no streams connected, buffer the data
   293→  if (!streams || streams.size === 0) {
   294→    if (!ptyDataBuffer.has(ptyId)) {
   295→      ptyDataBuffer.set(ptyId, [])
   296→    }
   297→    const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)!
   298→    buffer.push(data)
   299→    // Limit buffer size
   300→    if (buffer.length > 1000) {
   301→      buffer.shift()
   302→    }
   303→    return
   304→  }
   305→
   306→  const message = JSON.stringify({
   307→    type: 'data',
   308→    data
   309→  })
   310→
   311→  streams.forEach(ws => {
   312→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   313→      ws.send(message)
   314→    }
   315→  })
   316→}
   317→
   318→export function broadcastPtyExit(
   319→  ptyId: string,
   320→  code: number,
   321→  ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
   322→  terminalSubscriptions?: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
   323→): void {
   324→  const streams = ptyStreams.get(ptyId)
   325→  if (streams && streams.size > 0) {
   326→    const message = JSON.stringify({
   327→      type: 'exit',
   328→      code
   329→    })
   330→
   331→    streams.forEach(ws => {
   332→      if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   333→        ws.send(message)
   334→        ws.close(1000, 'PTY exited')
   335→      }
   336→    })
   337→  }
   338→
   339→  // Clean up stale subscriptions for this PTY
   340→  ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   341→  terminalSubscriptions?.delete(ptyId)
   342→}

   113→
   114→function handlePtyStreamUpgrade(
   115→  req: any,
   116→  socket: any,
   117→  head: any,
   118→  ptyId: string,
   119→  deps: WebSocketManagerDeps
   120→): void {
   121→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   122→  if (!ptyManager || !ptyManager.getProcess(ptyId)) {
   123→    log('PTY stream upgrade failed - PTY not found', { ptyId })
   124→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
   125→    socket.destroy()
   126→    return
   127→  }
   128→
   129→  const streamWss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
   130→
   131→  streamWss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
   132→    log('PTY stream connected', { ptyId })
   133→
   134→    const ptyStreams = deps.getPtyStreams()
   135→    if (!ptyStreams.has(ptyId)) {
   136→      ptyStreams.set(ptyId, new Set())
   137→    }
   138→    ptyStreams.get(ptyId)!.add(ws)
   139→
   140→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', ptyId }))
   141→
   142→    // Flush any buffered data
   143→    flushPtyBuffer(ptyId, ws, deps.getPtyDataBuffer())
   144→
   145→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
   146→      try {
   147→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
   148→
   149→        switch (msg.type) {
   150→          case 'input':
   151→            if (msg.data && ptyManager) {
   152→              ptyManager.write(ptyId, msg.data)
   153→            }
   154→            break
   155→
   156→          case 'resize':
   157→            if (msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   158→              ptyManager.resize(ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   159→            }
   160→            break
   161→
   162→          case 'ping':
   163→            ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   164→            break
   165→
   166→          default:
   167→            log('Unknown PTY stream message type', { type: msg.type, ptyId })
   168→        }
   169→      } catch (e) {
   170→        log('Invalid PTY stream message', { error: String(e), ptyId })
   171→      }
   172→    })
   173→
   174→    ws.on('close', () => {
   175→      log('PTY stream disconnected', { ptyId })
   176→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   177→      if (ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.size === 0) {
   178→        ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   179→      }
   180→    })
   181→
   182→    ws.on('error', (err) => {
   183→      log('PTY stream error', { error: String(err), ptyId })
   184→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   185→    })
   186→  })
   187→}
   188→
   189→function handleWebSocketMessage(ws: WebSocket, msg: any, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): void {
   190→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   191→  const terminalSubscriptions = deps.getTerminalSubscriptions()
   192→
   193→  switch (msg.type) {
   194→    case 'subscribe':
   195→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   196→        if (!terminalSubscriptions.has(msg.ptyId)) {
   197→          terminalSubscriptions.set(msg.ptyId, new Set())
   198→        }
   199→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)!.add(ws)
   200→        ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'subscribed', ptyId: msg.ptyId }))
   201→      }
   202→      break
   203→
   204→    case 'unsubscribe':
   205→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   206→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   207→      }
   208→      break
   209→
   210→    case 'write':
   211→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.data && ptyManager) {
   212→        ptyManager.write(msg.ptyId, msg.data)
   213→      }
   214→      break
   215→
   216→    case 'resize':
   217→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   218→        ptyManager.resize(msg.ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   219→      }
   220→      break
   221→
   222→    case 'ping':
   223→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   224→      break
   225→  }
   226→}
   227→
   228→function flushPtyBuffer(ptyId: string, ws: WebSocket, ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>): void {
   229→  const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)
   230→  if (!buffer || buffer.length === 0) return
   231→
   232→  buffer.forEach(data => {
   233→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   234→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'data', data }))
   235→    }
   236→  })
   237→
   238→  ptyDataBuffer.delete(ptyId)
   239→}
   240→
   241→function sendPendingFilesToClient(ws: WebSocket, pendingFiles: Map<string, PendingFile>): void {
   242→  const now = Date.now()
   243→  // Clean up expired files first
   244→  for (const [fileId, file] of pendingFiles) {
   245→    if (file.expiresAt < now) {
   246→      pendingFiles.delete(fileId)
   247→    }
   248→  }
   249→
   250→  const files = Array.from(pendingFiles.values()).map(f => ({
   251→    id: f.id,
   252→    name: f.name,
   253→    size: f.size,
   254→    mimeType: f.mimeType,
   255→    message: f.message
   256→  }))
   257→
   258→  if (files.length > 0) {
   259→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'files:pending', files }))
   260→  }
   261→}
   262→
   263→export function broadcastTerminalData(
   264→  ptyId: string,
   265→  data: string,
   266→  terminalSubscriptions: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
   267→): void {
   268→  const subscribers = terminalSubscriptions.get(ptyId)
   269→  if (!subscribers) return
   270→
   271→  const message = JSON.stringify({
   272→    type: 'terminal-data',
   273→    ptyId,
   274→    data
   275→  })
   276→
   277→  subscribers.forEach(ws => {
   278→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   279→      ws.send(message)
   280→    }
   281→  })
   282→}
   283→
   284→export function broadcastPtyData(
   285→  ptyId: string,
   286→  data: string,
   287→  ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
   288→  ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>
   289→): void {
   290→  const streams = ptyStreams.get(ptyId)
   291→
   292→  // If no streams connected, buffer the data
   293→  if (!streams || streams.size === 0) {
   294→    if (!ptyDataBuffer.has(ptyId)) {
   295→      ptyDataBuffer.set(ptyId, [])
   296→    }
   297→    const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)!
   298→    buffer.push(data)
   299→    // Limit buffer size
   300→    if (buffer.length > 1000) {
   301→      buffer.shift()
   302→    }
   303→    return
   304→  }
   305→
   306→  const message = JSON.stringify({
   307→    type: 'data',
   308→    data
   309→  })
   310→
   311→  streams.forEach(ws => {
   312→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   313→      ws.send(message)
   314→    }
   315→  })
   316→}
   317→
   318→export function broadcastPtyExit(
   319→  ptyId: string,
   320→  code: number,
   321→  ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
   322→  terminalSubscriptions?: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
   323→): void {
   324→  const streams = ptyStreams.get(ptyId)
   325→  if (streams && streams.size > 0) {
   326→    const message = JSON.stringify({
   327→      type: 'exit',
   328→      code
   329→    })
   330→
   331→    streams.forEach(ws => {
   332→      if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   333→        ws.send(message)
   334→        ws.close(1000, 'PTY exited')
   335→      }
   336→    })
   337→  }
   338→
   339→  // Clean up stale subscriptions for this PTY
   340→  ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   341→  terminalSubscriptions?.delete(ptyId)
   342→}
   343→

/**
 * Unified Task Interface
 *
 * Backend-agnostic task type used by the TaskPanel.
 * Both beads and kspec tasks normalize to this shape.
 */

export interface UnifiedTask {
  id: string
  displayId?: string
  title: string
  status: TaskStatus
  priority?: number
  type?: string
  description?: string
  created_at?: string
  updated_at?: string
  tags?: string[]
  automation?: AutomationEligibility
  // Source tracking (invisible to user, used internally for routing)
  _backend: 'beads' | 'kspec'
}

export type TaskStatus = 'open' | 'in_progress' | 'closed'

// Beads types: task, bug, feature, epic, chore
// Kspec types: task, bug, epic, spike, infra
export type TaskType = 'task' | 'bug' | 'feature' | 'epic' | 'chore' | 'spike' | 'infra'

export type AutomationEligibility = 'eligible' | 'needs_review' | 'manual_only'

export interface CreateTaskParams {
  title: string
  description?: string
  priority?: number
  type?: TaskType
  tags?: string
  automation?: AutomationEligibility
}

export interface UpdateTaskParams {
  status?: TaskStatus
  title?: string
  description?: string
  priority?: number
  automation?: AutomationEligibility
}

export type BackendKind = 'beads' | 'kspec' | 'none'

export interface BackendStatus {
  kind: BackendKind
  installed: boolean
  initialized: boolean
}

/**
 * TaskAdapter — the contract both backends implement.
 * The panel calls these methods without knowing which backend is active.
 */
export interface TaskAdapter {
  readonly kind: BackendKind

  check(cwd: string): Promise<BackendStatus>
  init(cwd: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
  list(cwd: string): Promise<UnifiedTask[]>
  show(cwd: string, taskId: string): Promise<UnifiedTask | null>
  create(cwd: string, params: CreateTaskParams): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
  start(cwd: string, taskId: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
  complete(cwd: string, taskId: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
  delete(cwd: string, taskId: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
  update(cwd: string, taskId: string, params: UpdateTaskParams): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
  cycleStatus(cwd: string, taskId: string, currentStatus: TaskStatus): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>

  watch(cwd: string): void
  unwatch(cwd: string): void
  onTasksChanged(callback: (data: { cwd: string }) => void): () => void
}

     5→import { Express, Request, Response } from 'express'
     6→import { WebSocket } from 'ws'
     7→import { validateProjectPath } from '../../mobile-security'
     8→import { log } from '../utils'
     9→import { LocalPty } from '../types'
    10→
    11→export function setupPtyRoutes(
    12→  app: Express,
    13→  getPtyManager: () => any,

    16→  getPtyDataBuffer: () => Map<string, string[]>,
    17→  broadcastPtyData: (ptyId: string, data: string) => void,
    18→  broadcastPtyExit: (ptyId: string, code: number) => void
    19→): void {
    20→  // Spawn a new PTY
    21→  app.post('/api/pty/spawn', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
    22→    try {
    23→      const { projectPath, sessionId, model, backend } = req.body
    24→
    25→      if (!projectPath || typeof projectPath !== 'string') {
    26→        return res.status(400).json({ error: 'projectPath is required' })
    27→      }
    28→
    29→      const pathValidation = validateProjectPath(projectPath)
    30→      if (!pathValidation.valid) {
    31→        return res.status(400).json({ error: pathValidation.error })
    32→      }
    33→      const safeProjectPath = pathValidation.normalizedPath!
    34→
    35→      const ptyManager = getPtyManager()
    36→      if (!ptyManager) {
    37→        return res.status(500).json({ error: 'PTY manager not available' })
    38→      }

    40→      log('PTY spawn request', { projectPath: safeProjectPath, sessionId, model, backend })
    41→
    42→      const ptyId = ptyManager.spawn(
    43→        safeProjectPath,
    44→        sessionId,
    45→        undefined, // autoAcceptTools
    46→        undefined, // permissionMode
    47→        model,
    48→        backend
    49→      )
    50→
    51→      // Track this PTY for cleanup
    52→      const localPty: LocalPty = {
    53→        ptyId,
    54→        projectPath: safeProjectPath,
    55→        dataCallbacks: new Set(),
    56→        exitCallbacks: new Set()
    57→      }
    58→      getLocalPtys().set(ptyId, localPty)
    59→
    60→      // Set up data forwarding to WebSocket streams
    61→      ptyManager.onData(ptyId, (data: string) => {
    62→        broadcastPtyData(ptyId, data)
    63→      })
    64→
    65→      // Set up exit handler
    66→      ptyManager.onExit(ptyId, (code: number) => {
    67→        log('PTY exited', { ptyId, code })
    68→        broadcastPtyExit(ptyId, code)
    69→        getLocalPtys().delete(ptyId)
    70→        getPtyStreams().delete(ptyId)
    71→        getPtyDataBuffer().delete(ptyId)
    72→      })
    73→
    74→      log('PTY spawned', { ptyId, projectPath })
    75→      res.json({ ptyId })
    76→    } catch (error) {
    77→      log('PTY spawn error', { error: String(error) })
    78→      res.status(500).json({ error: String(error) })
    79→    }
    80→  })
    81→

    83→  app.post('/api/pty/:id/write', (req: Request, res: Response) => {
    84→    try {
    85→      const { id } = req.params
    86→      const { data } = req.body
    87→
    88→      if (!data || typeof data !== 'string') {
    89→        return res.status(400).json({ error: 'data is required and must be a string' })
    90→      }

    92→      const ptyManager = getPtyManager()
    93→      if (!ptyManager) {
    94→        return res.status(500).json({ error: 'PTY manager not available' })
    95→      }
    96→
    97→      if (!ptyManager.getProcess(id)) {
    98→        return res.status(404).json({ error: 'PTY not found' })
    99→      }
   100→
   101→      ptyManager.write(id, data)
   102→      log('PTY write', { ptyId: id, dataLength: data.length })
   103→      res.json({ success: true })
   104→    } catch (error) {
   105→      log('PTY write error', { error: String(error) })
   106→      res.status(500).json({ error: String(error) })
   107→    }
   108→  })
   109→
   110→  // Resize PTY
   111→  app.post('/api/pty/:id/resize', (req: Request, res: Response) => {
   112→    try {

   130→        return res.status(404).json({ error: 'PTY not found' })
   131→      }

   134→      log('PTY resize', { ptyId: id, cols, rows })
   135→      res.json({ success: true })
   136→    } catch (error) {
   137→      log('PTY resize error', { error: String(error) })
   138→      res.status(500).json({ error: String(error) })
   139→    }
   140→  })

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive review of the changes:

## Comprehensive Review: `remotes/origin/fix/bug-persistence` Branch

Based on my thorough analysis of the codebase, here is my detailed review of the modifications across the seven files:

---

### 1. **src/main/session-store.ts**

**Current Structure:**
- `SessionStore` class manages persistent storage of workspace configuration via `workspace.json` with backup mechanism (`workspace.json.backup`)
- Core interface: `Workspace` containing `projects[]`, `openTabs[]`, `activeTabId`, `viewMode`, `tileLayout[]`, and `categories[]`
- Key method: `saveWorkspace(workspace)` at lines 214-225 implements a protection mechanism:
  - Rejects empty workspace saves if current workspace has projects (safeguard against data loss)
  - Calls `syncMetaProjects()` after save
  - **Note**: The method itself does NOT bypass the empty-workspace protection; all callers must respect this behavior

**Callers of `saveWorkspace()`:**
- `src/main/app/ipc-handlers/workspace.ts:13` - IPC handler `workspace:save` - passes entire workspace from renderer
- `src/main/mobile-server/routes/workspace.ts:60` - PUT `/api/workspace` endpoint - implements client-side validation at lines 49-59
- `src/main/mobile-server/routes/workspace.ts:84` - POST `/api/workspace` endpoint - implements client-side validation at lines 73-83

**Important Context:**
The route handlers implement **redundant protection** (lines 49-59, 73-83) before calling `saveWorkspace()`. This is intentional defense-in-depth. The method's own validation at lines 215-221 serves as a second safety net.

**Risk Assessment for Changes:**
- If the change removes or weakens the protection in `saveWorkspace()`, it creates a single point of failure since route validation could be bypassed or the method could be called from new callers
- If the change is to the protection logic itself, ensure it maintains backward compatibility with the route-level validations

---

### 2. **src/main/mobile-server/routes/workspace.ts**

**Current Structure:**
- Express routes for workspace and settings management: `/api/workspace`, `/api/settings`, `/api/sessions`, `/api/project/add`
- Three workspace endpoints:
  - `GET /api/workspace` (line 30) - reads and returns current workspace
  - `PUT /api/workspace` (line 43) - updates via direct workspace object in `req.body`
  - `POST /api/workspace` (line 67) - alternative update format expecting `req.body.workspace`
- Both PUT and POST implement separate validation protecting against empty workspace overwrites (lines 49-59 and 73-83)
- The validation pattern differs slightly between PUT (expects `req.body.projects`) and POST (expects `req.body.workspace.projects`)

**Validation Logic:**
- If incoming has 0 projects AND current has >0, reject with 400 error
- This mirrors the protection in `SessionStore.saveWorkspace()` but at the API boundary

**Risk Assessment for Changes:**
- The dual validation pattern (lines 49-59 vs 73-83) suggests both endpoints should remain synchronized
- If the change modifies workspace save behavior, ensure both endpoints are updated consistently
- The `/api/workspace/reload` endpoint (line 16) calls `reloadFromDisk()` directly, bypassing the save protection - this is correct as it's read-only

---

### 3. **src/main/mobile-server/websocket-manager.ts**

**Current Structure:**
- WebSocket server setup with support for two path types: `/ws` (main) and `/api/pty/:id/stream` (PTY streaming)
- Three broadcast functions:
  - `broadcastTerminalData()` (lines 263-282) - sends to terminal subscription set
  - `broadcastPtyData()` (lines 284-316) - sends to PTY streams with buffering for offline clients
  - **`broadcastPtyExit()`** (lines 318-342) - **signature: `(ptyId, code, ptyStreams, terminalSubscriptions?)`**

**Critical Function Analysis - `broadcastPtyExit()`:**
```typescript
export function broadcastPtyExit(
  ptyId: string,
  code: number,
  ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
  terminalSubscriptions?: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
): void
```

**Current Implementation (lines 318-342):**
- Takes 4 parameters (4th is optional)
- Sends exit message to ptyStreams subscribers
- Cleans up both `ptyStreams` and `terminalSubscriptions` (if provided)
- Line 341: `terminalSubscriptions?.delete(ptyId)` - uses optional chaining safely

**Current Callers:**
1. **`src/main/mobile-server/index.ts:216`** - wraps it in `broadcastPtyExit(ptyId, code, this.ptyStreams, this.terminalSubscriptions)` - passes all 4 params
2. **`src/main/mobile-server/routes/pty.ts:68`** - calls `broadcastPtyExit(ptyId, code)` with only 2 params via the wrapper in index.ts

**Risk Assessment for Signature Changes:**
- ✓ SAFE to make `terminalSubscriptions` optional (already is on line 322)
- ✓ SAFE to add new optional parameters at the end
- ✗ UNSAFE to remove parameters - line 216 in index.ts passes 4 arguments; removing any would break
- ✗ UNSAFE to change parameter order - callers depend on current order

**Specific Risk:** If the change modifies the signature (e.g., adds a required parameter or changes order), the call at index.ts:216 must be updated in parallel.

---

### 4. **src/main/orchestrator-api.ts**

**Current Structure:**
- HTTP server (not WebSocket) listening on port 19836 (ORCHESTRATOR_PORT)
- Supports only `127.0.0.1` (localhost) connections
- Three endpoint handlers:
  - `GET /sessions` - lists sessions
  - `GET /sessions/:id/output` - reads PTY output
  - `POST /sessions/:id/input` - sends input to PTY

**Key Method Analysis - `getProcess()` Usage:**
- **Line 128:** `const proc = this.ptyManager.getProcess(id)` - checks if PTY exists before writing input
- Returns early with 404 if process not found
- `PtyManager.getProcess(id)` signature: `getProcess(id: string): ClaudeProcess | undefined` (line 587 of pty-manager.ts)
- Returns `undefined` if process not found, which is safe for existence checks

**Risk Assessment:**
- ✓ Safe usage - checks for undefined before using
- Current code doesn't use the returned process object for anything except existence check
- If change involves modifying what `getProcess()` returns, ensure orchestrator-api properly handles both the existence check case and any new usage patterns

---

### 5. **src/renderer/components/beads/adapters/kspec-adapter.ts**

**Current Structure:**
- Client-side adapter implementing `TaskAdapter` interface for kspec backend
- Communicates with kspec daemon HTTP API on localhost:3456
- Key methods:
  - `check()` - verifies if kspec is initialized
  - `init()` - initializes kspec for a project
  - `list()`, `show()`, `create()`, `start()`, `complete()`, `delete()`, `update()` - standard CRUD
  - **`cycleStatus()`** (lines 211-224) - **STATUS CYCLING LOGIC**

**`cycleStatus()` Implementation:**
```typescript
async cycleStatus(cwd: string, taskId: string, currentStatus: TaskStatus): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
```
- **Input:** current status as `TaskStatus` ('open' | 'in_progress' | 'closed')
- **Logic:**
  - 'open' → calls `start()` (transitions to in_progress)
  - 'in_progress' → calls `complete()` (transitions to closed)
  - Default (closed) → sends PATCH with 'pending' status (back to open)
- Uses kspec-specific endpoints (`/start`, `/complete`, `/api/tasks/{id}` with PATCH)

**Interface Contract (from types.ts line 73):**
```typescript
cycleStatus(cwd: string, taskId: string, currentStatus: TaskStatus): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
```

**Caller Analysis:**
- `src/renderer/components/beads/useBeadsTasks.ts:339` - calls `adapter.cycleStatus(projectPath, taskId, currentStatus as UnifiedTask['status'])`
- The UI passes current status and expects the adapter to implement backend-specific lifecycle

**Risk Assessment:**
- ✓ Method signature matches interface contract
- ✓ All three status values handled
- ✓ Backend-specific logic is appropriate
- If change modifies the status cycling logic, ensure:
  - All three status transitions are still handled
  - The kspec-specific endpoint calls still match kspec API
  - Compatibility with BeadsAdapter (which uses generic PATCH in update())

---

### 6. **src/renderer/components/beads/useBeadsTasks.ts**

**Current Structure:**
- React hook managing task CRUD operations for beads/kspec backends
- Exports `TaskCrudCallbacks` and `TaskCrudState` interfaces
- Key callback: `handleCycleStatus()` (lines 321-350)

   103→export class SessionStore {
   104→  private configPath: string
   105→  private backupPath: string
   106→  private data: StoredData
   107→
   108→  constructor() {
   109→    const configDir = join(app.getPath('userData'), 'config')
   110→    if (!existsSync(configDir)) {
   111→      mkdirSync(configDir, { recursive: true })
   112→    }
   113→    this.configPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json')
   114→    this.backupPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json.backup')
   115→    this.data = this.load()
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  private loadFile(path: string): StoredData | null {
   119→    try {
   120→      if (!existsSync(path)) return null
   121→      const stat = statSync(path)
   122→      if (stat.size === 0) return null
   123→      const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
   124→      const data = JSON.parse(content)
   125→      if (!data?.workspace) return null
   126→      return data
   127→    } catch (e) {
   128→      console.error(`[SessionStore] Failed to load ${path}:`, e)
   129→      return null
   130→    }
   131→  }
   132→
   133→  private load(): StoredData {
   134→    // Try main file first
   135→    const main = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
   136→    if (main && (main.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   137→      return main
   138→    }
   139→
   140→    // Main file is empty/corrupt/missing — try backup
   141→    const backup = this.loadFile(this.backupPath)
   142→    if (backup && (backup.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   143→      console.log('[SessionStore] Main config empty/corrupt, restored from backup (' +
   144→        backup.workspace.projects.length + ' projects)')
   145→      // Restore backup as main file immediately
   146→      try {
   147→        writeFileSync(this.configPath, JSON.stringify(backup, null, 2))
   148→      } catch (e) {
   149→        console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to restore backup to main:', e)
   150→      }
   151→      return backup
   152→    }
   153→
   154→    // If main loaded but had 0 projects (valid empty state), use it
   155→    if (main) return main
   156→
   157→    return {
   158→      workspace: {
   159→        projects: [],
   160→        openTabs: [],
   161→        activeTabId: null
   162→      }
   163→    }
   164→  }
   165→
   166→  private save(): void {
   167→    try {
   168→      const json = JSON.stringify(this.data, null, 2)
   169→
   170→      // Validate we can parse what we're about to write
   171→      JSON.parse(json)
   172→
   173→      // Back up existing file before overwriting (if it has content)
   174→      if (existsSync(this.configPath)) {
   175→        try {
   176→          const stat = statSync(this.configPath)
   177→          if (stat.size > 0) {
   178→            // Only update backup if existing file is valid and has projects
   179→            const existing = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
   180→            if (existing && (existing.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   181→              writeFileSync(this.backupPath, readFileSync(this.configPath))
   182→            }
   183→          }
   184→        } catch (e) {
   185→          console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to update backup:', e)
   186→        }
   187→      }
   188→
   189→      // Atomic write: write to temp file, then rename
   190→      const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
   191→      writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
   192→
   193→      // Verify the temp file was written completely
   194→      const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
   195→      JSON.parse(written) // throws if truncated/corrupt
   196→
   197→      renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)
   198→    } catch (e) {
   199→      console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to save workspace:', e)
   200→    }
   201→  }

   203→  getWorkspace(): Workspace {

   205→  }
   206→
   207→  // Reload workspace from disk (useful when file was modified externally)
   208→  reloadFromDisk(): void {

   212→  }
   213→
   214→  saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): void {
   215→    // Protect against overwriting populated workspace with empty one
   216→    const incomingProjects = workspace?.projects?.length || 0
   217→    const currentProjects = this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
   218→    if (incomingProjects === 0 && currentProjects > 0) {
